steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: Paredes's core argument is that Mexican American folklore results from “ongoing interactions” of different cultures. “Ongoing” implies continuous, present-day mixing—not merely inherited from Spain's colonial past.

steps.title: Match the finding to the theory
steps.content:
Choice D directly supports this. Corridos of “clearly recent origin” show folklore being actively created recently—the hallmark of ongoing cultural interactions rather than colonial inheritance.

The other choices either highlight Spanish heritage (A) or don't demonstrate recent creation (B and C).

This matches choice D — D is correct.
